Off Calendar May-13-2024 Off Calendar

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of The Estate
Off Calendar Apr-15-2024 Continued To May-13-24 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Kristina Lam appeared by zoom for administrator.

The court noted that a petition has not been filed. Mrs. Lam stated that there will be a request for extraordinary fees, that they are finalizing that, and that they need two weeks. Status on close of estate is continued to May 13, 2024 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: Singh. Not reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of Estate
Off Calendar Jun-05-2023 Off Calendar

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of Estate
Off Calendar Apr-17-2023 Continued To Jun-05-23 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Kristina Lam appeared by zoom for administrator.

Mrs. Lam stated that they recently filed a supplemental inventory and appraisal for newly discovered assets, that they intend to file a first account, but the accounting cannot exceed two years, and that once they get the first accounting before the court, they will then file a final petition. Status on close of estate is continued to June 5, 2023 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Ronald E. Quidachay. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: Singh. Not reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of Estate
Off Calendar Jan-30-2023 Continued To Apr-17-23 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances via Zoom: Rowena Navia, Esq. for John Balquist (Administrator).

Ms. Navia stated that the majority of the estate is cryptocurrency, that this is taking a lot of time, that they filed an inventory in October, that they found additional cryptocurrency and a wallet, that they will be filing a supplemental inventory and appraisal, that they will most likely be filing a status report, and that she doesn't have a lot of experience with cryptocurrency. The Court stated that a digital wallet can present an interesting challenge. Ms. Navia stated that they will be filing a supplemental inventory and appraisal, that she is not sure if this will be below the threshold, that they hope they don't locate any more, but that they will continue to look.

At the conclusion of comments, the status on close of the estate is continued to April 17, 2023, at 9:00 a.m. in Dept. 204. The Court noted that Ms. Navia will probably be filing a petition to approve a further report on status of administration.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Deputy: Singh. Matter not reported.

Off Calendar Dec-12-2022 Continued To Jan-30-23 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Oct-27-2021 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Geoffrey Lee, via ZOOM, for Man Yu Lau Lam, Petitioner; Leticia Toledo, via ZOOM, for Cameron Gerhold, via ZOOM, Objector; Rowena Navia, via ZOOM, for John Balquist, proposed Administrator.

The court queries Ms. Toledo as to the status of notice to the Chinese Consulate. Ms. Toledo reports that the parties have an agreement to use a professional fiduciary and thus believes that the issue of notice to the Chinese Consulate is now moot. The court confirms with Ms. Toledo that her client has no issue with the court appointing John Balquist as Administrator. The court asks if anyone has any additional comments. No additional comments are presented.

The Second Amended Petition for Letters of Administration, nominating John Balquist as Administrator, is submitted.

Judge: Hon. Ross Moody. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: R. Rosado. Not reported.

***After the hearing, the competing petition for Letters of Administration, nominating Lucas Salinas as Administrator, is off calendar as the parties have agreed to the nomination of John Balquist as Administrator.
